title: Laser Flits

{{Quote|There is a local gang of teens called the Laser Flits. They are a relatively new gang operating here out of Mos Espa.|[[W. Wald|Wald]]|<ref name="SWG:WW" />}}
The '''Laser Flits''' or '''Laserflits''' were a gang which operated out of a [[Laser Flit Hangout|bunker]] on the edge of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]], a [[City/Legends|city]] located on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Dressed in distinctive purple [[Color/Legends|colored]] [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]], the armed gang of teenagers was responsible for occasional mischief. Sometime following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the Laser Flits ambushed a delivery of [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] parts. This prompted [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[W. Wald]] of [[Wald's Parts|Watto's Shop]] to hire a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] to attack the Laser Flits and recover the stolen parts for a customer of [[Gender/Legends|his]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] named [[Qord D'Baelor]].

==History==
{{Quote|I have the location of their hideout. Recently they ambushed a delivery of swoop parts we were delivering to a customer. There were ten parts in all. I need you to recover those parts. Most likely you'll find the parts on these gang members. If you have to teach these kids a lesson to get the parts back, so be it.|Wald|<ref name="SWG:WW" />}}
[[File:Laserflit.jpg|thumb|right|100px|A Human female Laser Flit with purple hair.]]
Sometime following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], a relatively new gang began operations out of a [[Laser Flit Hangout|bunker]] on the edge of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]], a [[City/Legends|city]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. The gang, known as the Laser Flits,<ref name="SWG:WW" /> shared a name with the [[flit]], an aggressive [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Reptavian/Legends|flying reptiles]] native to the world of [[Lok/Legends|Lok]].<ref name="TRoD">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Ruins of Dantooine]]''</ref> From their hangout in Mos Espa, the Laser Flits were known to engage in general mayhem.<ref name="SWG:WW" />

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> the Laser Flits ambushed a delivery of [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] parts from [[Wald's Parts|Watto's Shop]], a Mos Espa junk shop under the ownership the [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] of [[Watto/Legends|Watto]]. This resulted in a considerable amount of woe for the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[W. Wald]], an employee working for Watto. When Wald learned of the theft of his shipment, he tasked a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] under his employ with entering the Laser Flit Hangout, and forcibly recovering all ten of the stolen swoop parts. The spacer succeeded in recovering the parts, but not without [[Death/Legends|killing]] at least ten gang members during a violent [[Lightfight|blaster fight]]. Nevertheless, with the parts recovered, the delivery was finally completed when the spacer brought the shipment to [[Qord D'Baelor]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] customer who frequented the [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] inside [[Mos Espa Starport]].<ref name="SWG:WW" />

==Members==
{{Quote|There are only two extra jobs left so I'll let you choose which you do first. The first involves teaching a lesson to some punk kids here in Mos Espa.|Wald|<ref name="SWG:WW" />}}
The Laser Flits primarily comprised of teenagers, including a mix of male and female Humans, female [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]], and male [[Ishi Tib/Legends|Ishi Tib]]. All members of the gang wore some form of purple [[Color/Legends|colored]] [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]], sometimes mixed with shades of blue or gray. Different styles of their clothing included tactical skinsuits worn beneath sleeveless or rugged [[Jacket/Legends|jackets]], muscle [[shirt]]s worn with reinforced pants and cold weather jackets, or [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suits]]. Others wore more military-like garments, including basic camouflage pants and flex-form shirts worn beneath casual jackets with cargo pockets. Stylish dress uniform jackets worn with large pocket pants were another common dress combination. For protection, Laser Flits would often make use of [[Padded armor/Legends|padded armor]] [[Boot/Legends|boots]] and [[composite armor]] or [[Mabari armor]]weave [[Glove/Legends|gloves]]. The majority of the Human Laser Flit gang members sported avant-garde [[Hair/Legends|hairstyles]], with most [[Hair dye/Legends|dyeing]] their hair purple. Members of the gang were armed with multiple types of [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]] and [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrowers]], including [[DL-18 Blaster Pistol]]s, [[FWG-5 Flechette Smart Pistol]]s, [[Striker projectile pistol]]s, and [[SE-14 blaster pistol]]s.<ref name="SWG:WW" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Laser Flits appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:WW" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> They were added to the game as part of a quest titled "[[W:c:swg:Wald's Woes|Wald's Woes]]" with the release of "[[W:c:swg:Chapter 2|Chapter 2: The Talus Incident]]," on [[August 1]], [[2006]].<ref name="SonyForumsCite-Chapter 2: The Talus Incident Publish Notes, August 1st">{{SonyForumsCite|full_url=http://soe.lithium.com/swg/board/message?board.id=DevDiscussion&message.id=58871|text="Chapter 2: The Talus Incident" Publish Notes, August 1st}}</ref> Players who performed the quest were tasked with entering the Laser Flit Hangout and killing Laser Flit gang members until recovering ten stolen swoop parts. While W. Wald referred to the gang as "Laser Flits" with a space between the two words, there were instances within the game where the gang was referred to as "Laserflits" without the space. For example, targeting or examining [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] members of the gang identified them as "Laserflit gang members."<ref name="SWG:WW" />
